test.command: docker compose exec funasr bash -c 'cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -x -v'

typecheck.command: docker compose exec funasr bash -c 'cd /app/backend && ty check .'

lint.command: docker compose exec funasr bash -c 'cd /app/backend && ruff format --check . && ruff check .'

frontend-check.command: docker compose exec funasr bash -c 'cd /app/frontend && pnpm check'

frontend-format.command: docker compose exec funasr bash -c 'cd /app/frontend && pnpm format:check'
frontend-format.detail: All matched files use Prettier code style

reviewsSummary.note: Re-run: fix-fts5-triggers feature is still pending (not implemented). No new completed features to review. Blocking issue from round 1 persists.

blockingIssues.description: FTS5 segment triggers use the memo's rowid as the FTS rowid. In a contentless FTS5 table (content=''), each rowid maps to exactly one entry. When a second segment is inserted for the same memo, the trigger deletes the existing FTS entry and replaces it with only the new segment's text. This means only the LAST segment per memo is searchable — all previous segments' text is lost from the FTS index. Example: if memo has segments 'Hello world' and 'Goodbye world', only 'Goodbye world' will be findable via FTS MATCH.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Create migration 002 that drops and recreates the FTS triggers to use the segment's own rowid instead of the memo's rowid. Each segment gets its own FTS entry. Update test_fts5_basic_search to insert multiple segments per memo and verify ALL are searchable via FTS MATCH.

nonBlockingIssues.description: || true error suppression in Dockerfile pip/pnpm install commands could mask real dependency issues
NANOSCRIBE_STATIC_DIR=/app/static set in docker-compose but directory doesn't exist in dev stage
Duplicated DB connection helpers in core/dependencies.py vs db/__init__.py
health_check() triggers GPU detection on every call via get_capabilities()
DATA_DIR env var reading not centralized through Settings config
Path traversal risk in serve_spa() catch-all — candidate.resolve() should verify containment within STATIC_DIR
Placeholder favicon.png contains text, not actual image
Pre-commit hook omits ty check and svelte-check for speed (incomplete check vs make check)
File hygiene sed doesn't filter by file extension, operates on all staged files

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add documentation for the SPA serving pattern: FastAPI serves SvelteKit adapter-static output (200.html fallback, _app/ immutable assets) via catch-all route. Workers modifying main.py should understand the mount ordering and SvelteKit output conventions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m1-sveltekit-skeleton review flagged that backend/app/main.py:30-53 implements SPA serving with SvelteKit adapter-static conventions not documented in AGENTS.md.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the Svelte 5 state management convention: use runes ($state, $derived, $effect) with getter functions for stores rather than traditional writable/readable stores. Multiple features will add stores and should follow the same pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m1-sveltekit-skeleton review noted capabilities.svelte.ts uses $state with getter functions, a valid but specific Svelte 5 pattern that should be consistent across the codebase.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Centralize configuration: all env vars (NANOSCRIBE_DATA_DIR, NANOSCRIBE_STATIC_DIR) should be read through core/config.py Settings class, not duplicated at module level across multiple files.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews of m1-fastapi-skeleton and m1-sveltekit-skeleton flagged duplicated env var reading in main.py, api/system.py, and core/config.py.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker created library doc .factory/library/sqlite-schema.md but it wasn't committed
services.yaml doesn't mention Makefile targets as canonical way to run checks
Pre-commit hook file hygiene convention not in AGENTS.md
Ruff lint rules not documented in AGENTS.md
